name: Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Rochester
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency in Rochester, NY offering debt management, credit counseling, and housing advice for over 50 years. Free consultations and certified counselors.
description_long:
CCCS of Rochester is a locally-based, non-profit credit counseling organization that has served the Rochester area for over 50 years. According to their website, they are "the only locally-based non-profit Credit Counseling Agency in the Rochester area" and have helped hundreds of thousands of people restore their financial health during that period.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of financial services including debt management programs, credit and budget counseling, housing counseling (with certified housing counselors), first-time home buyer assistance, and reverse mortgage guidance. They provide a "My Virtual Counselor" tool that allows consumers to estimate savings on a debt management plan before meeting with a counselor. All initial consultations are offered for free, with both online appointment scheduling and phone consultation available at (585) 546-3440.

CCCS of Rochester distinguishes itself through its non-profit status, community focus, and use of certified counselors. They emphasize operating as a "no judgement zone" and highlight that referrals from satisfied clients are their #1 source of new business. The organization also conducts community financial literacy programs in local schools, as evidenced by testimonials from guidance counselors.

The company relies on client testimonials and word-of-mouth rather than aggressive marketing. While their services appear effective based on documented success stories (including one client who paid off $20,000 in credit card debt over 4 years), prospective clients should understand that debt management plans require commitment over multiple years and may involve negotiating reduced interest rates with creditors rather than eliminating debt entirely.
address: 1050 University Ave Suite A, Rochester, NY 14607

company_info.headquarters: 1050 University Ave Suite A, Rochester, NY 14607

pros: Confirmed non-profit status with 50+ years of local service in Rochester area
Free initial credit counseling and budget assessment consultations available online and by phone
Certified housing counselors on staff for foreclosure avoidance and first-time homebuyer programs
Debt management plans with negotiated creditor rates to reduce payment amounts and stop collector calls
No-judgment approach explicitly stated; emphasis on community relationships and referral-based growth
Virtual counselor tool allows self-assessment of debt management plan savings before formal consultation
Strong documented success stories with multi-year client outcomes (e.g., $20,000 credit card payoff)
cons: Debt management plans require multi-year commitment (testimonial mentions 4 years for $20,000 debt)
Limited geographic focus to Rochester, NY area; unclear if services available outside region
Website does not disclose specific fees, creditor participation rates, or terms for debt management plans
No information provided on average time to complete programs or typical creditor interest rate reductions
No details on bankruptcy counseling or alternatives if debt management plan is not suitable
best_for: Individuals with multiple credit cards maxed out seeking affordable debt consolidation and payment reduction
First-time homebuyers in Rochester area needing down payment assistance and homeownership education
Homeowners at risk of foreclosure seeking certified housing counseling and loss mitigation options
People with budget management struggles wanting personalized guidance from certified financial counselors
diagnosis: CCCS of Rochester is best for Rochester-area residents with credit card debt or housing concerns who qualify for non-profit counseling and can commit to multi-year repayment plans. Primary caveat: debt management programs require sustained monthly payments over several years and depend on creditor participation—this is not a quick fix or debt elimination service, but rather a structured debt reduction strategy for those who cannot manage payments independently.
services: Free debt management programs with negotiated creditor payment reductions
Credit and budget counseling with certified counselors
Housing counseling with certified housing counselors
First-time homebuyer education and assistance programs
Reverse mortgage consultation and equity leverage education
Free initial online and phone consultations
My Virtual Counselor debt savings calculator tool
Creditor call negotiation and payment plan coordination
Financial literacy programs for school students and community groups
